items.title: Layers of experiment reproducibility
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  seedNode["Random seeds"]
  dataNode["Data version and split"]
  codeNode["Code commit"]
  envNode["Pinned environment"]
  runNode["Training run"]
  metricsNode["Logged metrics"]
  seedNode --> runNode
  dataNode --> runNode
  codeNode --> runNode
  envNode --> runNode
  runNode --> metricsNode
items.caption: Seeds are one layer. Comparable reruns also need the same data, code, and runtime stack.

items.title: RNG streams in a typical training script
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  pyNode["Python random"]
  npNode["NumPy RNG"]
  torchNode["PyTorch CPU/GPU"]
  splitNode["sklearn random_state"]
  loaderNode["DataLoader shuffle"]
  initNode["Weight init"]
  pyNode --> initNode
  npNode --> splitNode
  torchNode --> initNode
  torchNode --> loaderNode
items.caption: Multiple independent RNGs must each be seeded; setting only one library often leaves hidden randomness.

items.title: Re-run verification workflow
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  logNode["Read logged seed + commit + env"]
  checkoutNode["Checkout commit and restore env"]
  seedNode["Apply same seeds"]
  trainNode["Re-run training"]
  compareNode["Compare metrics"]
  investigateNode["Investigate drift if mismatch"]
  logNode --> checkoutNode
  checkoutNode --> seedNode
  seedNode --> trainNode
  trainNode --> compareNode
  compareNode --> investigateNode
items.caption: Experiment trackers store metadata; reproducibility practice is rerunning and validating that metadata was complete.
